title: Explain Runtime
short_answer: Runtime is where the app writes live working files.
text: Runtime is the app's active working area. It stores queues, status files, latest responses, speech output, logs, state files, and temporary working records.

title: Explain State
short_answer: State files store the current condition of the app.
text: State files tell the app what is currently active, enabled, running, or recently updated. They are useful for debugging what the system thinks is happening.

title: Explain System Runtime
short_answer: system_runtime.json stores the main runtime state of the app.
text: system_runtime.json holds active_mode, active_persona, service flags, edit mode, and the help explain capture flag. It is the main place to check current system-level runtime state.

title: Explain Mode
short_answer: Mode describes the current behavior level of the assistant.
text: Mode is a runtime setting such as passive, listening_light, smart_background, or fully_active. Today it is partly informational, but it is intended to control how active the system should be.

title: Explain Response Queue
short_answer: response_queue.jsonl sends spoken questions to spoken_queries.
text: response_queue.jsonl is where router_dispatch places events meant for response runners. spoken_queries reads this queue when target_runner is spoken_queries.

title: Explain Spoken Query Results
text: This JSONL file records the result produced by spoken_queries, including answer_text, speech_text, runner name, status, and metadata.

title: Explain Chat History
short_answer: chat_history.jsonl stores user and assistant conversation records.
text: chat_history.jsonl is useful for history, UI, memory, and debugging. It should store clean records of what the user asked and what the assistant answered.

title: Explain Latest Response
short_answer: latest_response.json stores the most recent assistant response.
text: latest_response.json is a quick file to check when debugging whether the assistant produced a final answer. If it is empty, speech output may have nothing to say.

title: Explain Speech Out
short_answer: speech_out contains files used to speak assistant responses.
text: speech_out is the runtime area for TTS queues, generated audio, and spoken history. Its status lives under runtime/status/speech_out and playback state under runtime/state/speech_out.

title: Explain Jsonl
short_answer: JSONL stores one JSON record per line.
text: JSONL is useful for queues, logs, histories, and append-only runtime records. The app uses it when new events or results must be added over time.
